properties.titulo_evento: Germany scraps F126 frigate program, pivots to MEKO warships amid cost and contractor chaos
properties.descricao: Germany Navy leaders have already blessed the fallback ships as capable of meeting NATO capability requirements, but an alliance deadline looms.

properties.titulo_evento: Tank maker KNDS plans stock market listing, Germany to buy 40% stake
properties.descricao: The firm showed a proposed battle tank at Eurosatory defense exhibition in Paris boasting a French turret and gun mounted on a German Leopard 2 chassis.

properties.titulo_evento: US Senate joins House in voting to halt Iran war
properties.descricao: It marks the first time both chambers approved directing a president to remove armed forces from hostilities since the War Powers Act was adopted in 1973.

properties.titulo_evento: Frustrating Israel, fiber-optic killer drone technology has arrived in southern Lebanon
properties.descricao: Analysts believe that any military engaged in conflict will, in one form or another, encounter technologies first seen in Ukraine.

properties.titulo_evento: Top European leaders vow ‘strong’ support for Ukraine ahead of NATO summit

properties.titulo_evento: UN nuclear chief says Iran inspections will happen, Tehran says after deal
properties.descricao: Head of the IAEA, Rafael Grossi, said the MoU stated 'explicitly' that the agency would supervise inspections in Iran.

properties.titulo_evento: Why does Israel want to be less dependent on US weapons?
properties.descricao: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has called for Israel to pursue 'armaments independence'.

properties.descricao: Presidential candidate Ivan Cepeda accepted the victory of his opponent Abelardo de la Espriella.

properties.titulo_evento: Palestinian boy amongst dead in Israeli strike on displaced tent in Gaza
properties.descricao: An Israeli drone strike on a tent sheltering displaced Palestinians in Khan Younis killed two people, including a child.

properties.titulo_evento: Did Brexit actually work?
properties.descricao: A vote that changed Britain forever. Ten years after Brexit, the UK is still grappling with its consequences.

properties.titulo_evento: Iran’s president awarded honorary doctorate in Pakistan
properties.descricao: Iran’s President Masoud Pezeshkian was awarded an honorary medical fellowship in cardiac surgery.

properties.titulo_evento: The children Gaza lost
properties.descricao: What does a new UN report documenting the killing and suffering of Palestinian children in Gaza reveal?

properties.titulo_evento: Power outages hit France as it records hottest day since measurements began
properties.descricao: France has recorded it hottest day since records began in 1947, its national weather agency says, breaking a record set on Tuesday.

properties.titulo_evento: UN nuclear chief says inspectors will visit Iran sites as part of war deal
properties.descricao: Rafael Grossi says the IAEA is "working on modalities" but an Iranian  minister says such access would only be part of a final deal with the US.

properties.descricao: Brad Lander unseats Dan Goldman in a race that laid bare the city's divisions over the Israel-Gaza war.

properties.titulo_evento: Biggest city in Crimea without power after Ukraine strikes
properties.descricao: Sevastopol's Moscow-installed governor warns there will be no electricity in some areas until the evening.

properties.titulo_evento: Colombia's left-wing presidential candidate concedes defeat

properties.titulo_evento: Congress passes war powers measure for first time, breaking with Trump over Iran
properties.descricao: Experts say Trump is likely to ignore it, but that even though it carries little power, it is politically significant.

properties.titulo_evento: Dozens of ships head through Strait of Hormuz after US-Iran deal
properties.descricao: Traffic in the waterway has risen since the US and Iran signed a deal aimed at ending the war, including 42 ships on Saturday alone.

properties.descricao: Rail company Deutsche Bahn had to pause train services across the country for more than two-and-a-half hours.

properties.titulo_evento: Why are there holiday delay warnings over the EU's new border system?
properties.descricao: The EU's much-delayed Entry/Exit System will change the way UK passengers travel to 29 countries.

properties.titulo_evento: Tehran labels framework deal 'US defeat' as Trump threatens to halt talks
properties.descricao: Iranian key negotiator hailed the US–Iran framework deal as a “declaration of US defeat,” while Trump warned that talks would end immediately if reports that Tehran is seeking to impose tolls on ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz prove to be true.

properties.titulo_evento: Kazakhstan signs €10bn in deals with Europe as Tokayev wraps Brussels visit
properties.descricao: During President Kassym-Jomart Tokayev's visit to Brussels, strategic documents were signed with the EU, and 30 commercial deals were concluded with EU companies, with the EU being the Central Asian country's largest trading and investment partner.

properties.titulo_evento: IAEA chief says inspections of Iran's nuclear sites 'going to happen'
properties.descricao: IAEA's Grossi pushed back against Tehran's claim that no agreement has been reached to allow inspectors to inspect bombed enrichment sites, citing the signed US-Iran framework deal.

properties.titulo_evento: Giant Messi statue honors Argentina's World Cup hero
properties.descricao: A giant mural near Buenos Aires and a towering statue in Patagonia highlight Lionel Messi's enduring status as a symbol of unity and pride for Argentines during the World Cup.

properties.titulo_evento: Belgian royals roll out red carpet for Japan's emperor
properties.descricao: Japanese Emperor Naruhito and Empress Masako received a ceremonial welcome at the Royal Palace of Brussels as they began a state visit to Belgium.

properties.titulo_evento: UN launches Hormuz evacuation for 11,000 stranded seafarers
properties.descricao: The operation follows a peace agreement between the United States and Iran that ended months of conflict and allowed commercial shipping to resume through one of the world's most important maritime routes.

properties.titulo_evento: Europe leaders reaffirm unity on defence ahead of Ankara NATO summit
properties.descricao: European leaders met Wednesday in Berlin ahead of next month's NATO summit in Ankarai to discuss European defence and security guarantees for Ukraine, following tensions with the United States and among the main European members.

properties.titulo_evento: Germany's Merz rallies European NATO allies ahead of summit
properties.descricao: Chancellor Friedrich Merz hosted leaders of European NATO powers in Berlin to hash out consensus on bolstering European defense. With US tensions looming, the leaders signaled that transatlantic ties are improving.

properties.titulo_evento: What does British PM's resignation mean for European security?
properties.descricao: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer was widely known for his support of Ukraine and his efforts towards a new EU–UK defense partnership. What will happen to those policies now that Starmer has announced his resignation?

properties.titulo_evento: Iran after the war — fragile peace, shattered economy
properties.descricao: The US has temporarily allowed Iran to export oil and indicated it will provide billions to relieve the crisis triggered by the war. With ordinary Iranians at their limit, however, Tehran already fears new protests.

properties.titulo_evento: Climate change: Heatwave costs the German economy billions
properties.descricao: Never before has a June been as hot in Germany as in 2026. This has consequences not only for people and nature, but also for businesses. Heat reduces productivity and causes illness, while cooling is expensive.

properties.titulo_evento: Southeast Asia boost defense ties but avoids firm alliances
properties.descricao: Countries across the region are expanding defense cooperation in ways that change the South China Sea equation but fall short of a new anti-China bloc.

properties.titulo_evento: Trump offers Iran an economic lifeline — with strings
properties.descricao: Iran has seen a speedy rebound in oil exports after the US-Iran deal granted immediate waivers on US sanctions. Relief on frozen assets and a $300 billion reconstruction fund now hinge on Tehran meeting key conditions.

properties.titulo_evento: North Korean soldier crosses border to South
properties.descricao: South Korea's military says it is investigating the soldier's reasons for crossing the inner-Korean border. While dozens of North Koreans flee each year, usually via China, crossing directly south is very rare.

properties.titulo_evento: Iran nuclear inspections 'going to happen,' says IAEA head
properties.descricao: After contradicting US and Iranian statements, the UN nuclear agency said inspections of Iran's nuclear sites would take place. Iran's top negotiator called the US-Iran deal "America's declaration of defeat." More at DW.

properties.titulo_evento: US: Senate vote calls for an end to Trump's Iran war in rare bipartisan rebuke
properties.descricao: Trump criticized the largely symbolic Senate resolution as "meaningless" after several Republicans joined Democrats in a rare break with the White House.

properties.titulo_evento: Germany news: Major warship project to be scrapped
properties.descricao: The Defense Ministry is dropping a multi-billion-euro project to build F126 frigates, media say. And train services have resumed after a communications glitch overnight halted Germany's rail network . DW has more.

properties.titulo_evento: Poland, Ukraine: Dispute between two close allies deepens
properties.descricao: Polish President Karol Nawrocki last week stripped Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy of Poland's highest state honor. How did it come to this? And what has it got to do with next year's election in Poland?

properties.titulo_evento: Ukraine says major Crimea bridge destroyed in latest attack
properties.descricao: Ukraine continues to hit infrastructure on the Crimea to isolate the Russian-annexed peninsula. Meanwhile, Russia attacked Volodymyr Zelenskyy's hometown with a ballistic missile in a deadly strike.
